chapter_title.ar: باب الشَّرْطِ وَ الْخِيَارِ فِي الْبَيْعِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 70 – The stipulation (of returning goods) and the choice regarding (not transacting) the sale

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/165)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/165)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ بَايَعْتُ رَجُلًا فَلَمَّا بَايَعْتُهُ قُمْتُ فَمَشَيْتُ خِطَاءً ثُمَّ رَجَعْتُ إِلَى مَجْلِسِي لِيَجِبَ الْبَيْعُ حِينَ افْتَرَقْنَا .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abu Ayoub, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,

‘I heard Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> was sold (something) by a man. So when he had sold it, I<sup>asws</sup> arose, walked some steps, then I<sup>asws</sup> returned to my<sup>asws</sup> seat, in order for the sale to become binding when we separated’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from abu Ayyub from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “I heard abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘I once purchased something from someone; then I got up and walked a few steps, then returned to the meeting place so that the deal becomes binding.’”
